text:
This is a delicate place; here is my honest read.

**1. Per-slice $\Fill_R(Z_y)\le R_1E_y+E_y^2/S_1$ is *false*; LE-HAF must use the parametric structure.** A fiber can have $\Mass(Z_y)\gg E_y$ when $\lambda=\|dG|_T\|_{op}\approx 0$ (flap), while still being centrally placed and thus having $\Fill_R(Z_y)\gtrsim S_1S_2S_3$. So no pointwise source-push argument will work. LE-HAF must rely on (a) compatibly chosen $B_y$ across $y$ and (b) the $\mathcal{B}_z$ averaging swallowing flap mass *statistically*. Any direct deformation that treats one slice at a time will not give the right linear $E_y$ term.

**2. The bad set is brittle as written.** $\mathcal{B}_z$ uses *coordinate* plaques and the Fubini bound $|F(P_{ij}(x))|\le R_iR_j\le R_3R_4$. A flap that is a 2-disk tilted off the coordinate axes does not lie on any one coordinate plaque, and the $R_3R_4/q$ probability is not obviously achieved. Natural fix: coarse-grain via a random cubulation $\Gamma$ at scale $\ell$ and define $\mathcal{B}_z^{\text{cell}}$ as a union of whole 4-cells whose coordinate 2-faces have $F$-image meeting $z$. Then the plaque Fubini holds rigorously, but the cost of replacing $Z_y$ by its FF-pushforward onto $\Gamma^{(2)}$ has to be charged against $R_1E_y$ or $E_y^2/S_1$. That is exactly where the argument either succeeds or fails.

**3. Seam at $\partial\mathcal{B}_z$.** Sharp restriction $B_y\llcorner\mathcal{B}_z$ produces $\partial(B_y\llcorner\mathcal{B}_z)$ that can be much larger than the bad mass. The clean fix is *not* to cut $B_y$, but to build $B_y$ in two layers: an inner layer supported in a thickened $\mathcal{B}_z$, and an outer layer supported in the punctured complement, joined along an interior 2-surface obtained by pulling back a retraction $Q\setminus\{z\}\to\partial Q$. The cost of the outer retraction is $\sim E_y\cdot\text{length of retraction}/S_1\sim E_y^2/S_1$ after Cauchy–Schwarz, which is exactly the second LE-HAF term. The $R_1E_y$ term is then the cost of closing the inner layer to the $x_1$-face by an $x_1$-push of the *image* under $G$, pulled back — and this is the place where weighting by $\lambda^2$ instead of by mass becomes natural (the push acts on $G_\#Z_y$ in target, not on $Z_y$ in source).

**Concrete construction I would try.** Randomized cubulation $\Gamma$ at a scale $\ell=\ell(E_y)$, two-layer filling as above, with the cuts taken at coordinate faces. The technical question is whether the $G$-pull-back retraction respects the $\Dil_2$ relation $\lambda^2J_2F\le 1$ tightly enough to give the linear $E_y$ rather than $\Mass(Z_y)$. I have not checked this.

**Genuine obstruction check.** The stress model $S=(1,1,L,L)$, $R=(L^{-1/5},L^{13/15},L^{13/15},L^{13/15})$ violates LE-HAF if realizable. I do not see how to realize it via Chapter 8 maps after the round-25 retraction, but I would press harder on non-Guth constructions: in particular, a Pontryagin–Thom collapse from a $\Dil_2$-bounded embedding $S\hookrightarrow R$ where the boundary obstructions are evaded by a non-rectangular embedding source. The exponent $13/15$ being exactly the equality case of Estimate 1 at $(j,k,l)=(1,2,4)$ is suspicious and might be where a new construction lives.

**Probability assessment.** ~55% LE-HAF or a close variant holds and needs a $\Dil_2$-weighted Federer–Fleming; ~30% the theorem holds by a different route (a directional rational-homotopy invariant adapted to $(f_3,f_4)$/$(f_1,f_2)$); ~15% the theorem itself fails. The $(S_2/S_3)^{1/10}$ gap is small enough to look like a missing technique, not a fundamental obstruction. I would write the partial bound $S_1S_2^{3/5}S_3^{7/5}S_4$ as the theorem and state LE-HAF as an explicit conjecture if no progress in 2–3 weeks.
